data.description:
## Why
3183 (change 580) delivered the merge-gate + snapshot ledger with a divergent schema
and verdict set vs. the APPROVED epic design. Approved AC8 requires the SNAPSHOT
authorship ledger to record `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature, signer_pubkey,
position}`; the delivered ledger records `{event_uuid, author_id, author_sig, epoch}`.
Approved AC9 requires a distinct `key_not_valid_at_era` verdict + a
`verified/unverified/unsigned` display grouping; the delivered code folds the
revoked-key case into `bad-signature`/`unknown-author` (a revoked-key event is
indistinguishable from a forged one). This story transitions to the approved schema +
verdict. Depends on TS1 (content_hash) + TS2 (commit-anchored position). (User rejected
the delivered design; see session_log d7c0.)

## What
- **Ledger (compact.py):** the SNAPSHOT `authorship_ledger` records
  `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature, signer_pubkey, position}` per signed folded
  event, where `signer_pubkey` is the actual public key (self-contained — verify without
  re-resolving the identity keyring), `content_hash` is TS1's digest, and `position` is
  `(commit_sha, intra_commit_index)` (TS2's commit anchor). This makes a compacted
  event verifiable IN ISOLATION.
- **Verdict (verify_authorship + schema):** add `key_not_valid_at_era` (signature is
  cryptographically valid but the signing key was NOT valid at the event's commit —
  distinct from `bad-signature`=forged and `unknown-author`=no identity). Update
  `verify_signature_result.schema.json`'s enum. Document the `verified / unverified
  (bad-signature | key_not_valid_at_era) / unsigned` display grouping.
- **verify-authorship** rewired to read the new ledger schema (verify from
  signer_pubkey + content_hash directly) and to emit `key_not_valid_at_era`.

## Scope
In: the approved ledger schema, the `key_not_valid_at_era` verdict + enum + grouping,
and verify-authorship reading the new ledger. Out: authorship payload (TS1), era model
(TS2). NOTE: replaces the delivered `{author_id, author_sig, epoch}` ledger + the
4-way verdict without key_not_valid_at_era.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The SNAPSHOT authorship ledger records `{event_uuid, content_hash, signature,
  signer_pubkey, position}` per signed folded event; a round-trip test signs events,
  compacts, and asserts `verify-authorship` returns `verified` from the ledger ALONE
  (raw event objects gone, identity keyring not re-resolved).
- [ ] `verify-authorship` emits a distinct `key_not_valid_at_era` verdict for an event
  whose signature is valid but whose key was not valid at the event's commit (test),
  distinct from `bad-signature` and `unknown-author`.
- [ ] `verify_signature_result.schema.json` includes `key_not_valid_at_era`; docs
  record the verified/unverified/unsigned grouping (test asserts the enum).

## file_impact
- `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py` (approved ledger schema)
- `src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py` (read new ledger; key_not_valid_at_era verdict)
- `src/rebar/schemas/verify_signature_result.schema.json` (verdict enum)
data.title: transition: snapshot ledger schema + key_not_valid_at_era verdict (approved AC8/AC9)
